tweets.text: .@StateDeptSpox discusses today’s @UN Security Council meeting on Ukraine and Russia that included remarks by @USAmbUN: “We’ll be looking for concrete signs of de-escalation.” https://t.co/W5RsSQu3mT
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the State Department. Also streaming live on https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz and https://t.co/3tGO6yHMR0. https://t.co/HNAHM1Y9AE

FACT: It was Russia that invaded Ukraine in 2014, occupies Crimea, controls armed forces in the Donbas region of Ukraine, and has now amassed more than 100,000 troops on the border with Ukraine. https://t.co/OQBFzSjLws

Today, #COVAX provided 203,580 Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to the West Bank and Gaza facilitated by the United States. We are committed to helping Palestinians and others around the world battle COVID-19 in partnership with @gavi.

The United States sends best wishes to those around the world celebrating the #LunarNewYear. During the #YearoftheTiger, we wish you a year of opportunity, success, and good health. https://t.co/GASkgVMQnW https://t.co/XUNFtL5jAO

.@UnderSecStateP: This is a moment for diplomacy and for cool heads to prevail. That’s what we want. However, if President Putin rejects the peaceful negotiated path that we have offered, we must and we will be prepared. https://t.co/eCi4zsGwBg
This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to deliver more than 4 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to five countries in Africa. https://t.co/Hs4piCzlB5

.@SecBlinken: If Russia invades and occupies Ukraine, what’s next? It may seem like a distant regional dispute or yet another example of Russian bullying. But at stake are principles that have made the world safer and more stable for decades. https://t.co/NQExhRqV6n

.@DeputySecState: It bears repeating, that it was Russia that invaded Ukraine in 2014 and occupies Crimea to this day. It is Russia that continues to fuel a war in eastern Ukraine that has claimed more than 14,000 lives and destroyed entire towns. https://t.co/Mkl08Ic6vE

Today the U.S. delivered over 1.5 million doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to the people of Morocco. We are working with our global partners and @gavi #COVAX to deliver safe and effective vaccines around the world. https://t.co/6P6ylqKqWL

.@SecBlinken: There should be no doubt about our seriousness of purpose when it comes to diplomacy, and we’re acting with equal focus and force to bolster Ukraine’s defenses and prepare a swift, united response to further Russian aggression. More: https://t.co/Urj6L0jj4N. https://t.co/73BdHDvHu7
.@SecBlinken: We’ve given Russia two paths. https://t.co/VhF9bXOB5o

U.S.-Maldives ties grow stronger by helping each other in times of global crisis. The United States donated an additional 210,600 Pfizer vaccine doses to Maldives via #COVAX. These vaccine doses are vital to slowing the spread of the virus and saving lives. https://t.co/B78KMEOLXj

The United States, in partnership with #COVAX, delivered 100,620 doses of the COVID-19 Pfizer vaccine to the people of Moldova. We reaffirm our commitment to saving lives worldwide and helping end this pandemic. https://t.co/JtMJ0xGWPi

Today the U.S. delivered 310,050 doses of the Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine to the people of Tunisia, bringing the total doses delivered to Tunisia to over 2.8 million. In December, @POTUS pledged to deliver 200 million vaccine doses in 100 days around the world. @gavi #COVAX https://t.co/1KvVxqKdgy

“We call on all major energy producer countries to join us in ensuring world energy markets are stable and well-supplied.” The 🇺🇸 and 🇪🇺 are working together on energy security cooperation. Joint Statement from @POTUS and @EU_Commission President: https://t.co/HrN2BvPmC9

In today’s briefing, @StateDeputySpox took questions on Afghanistan, Russia, Ukraine & the situation in Burkina Faso: “We condemn the actions by military officers who claim to have dissolved the government/national assembly and suspended the constitution.” https://t.co/FBmkZI9He0

“I was considered a troublemaker, and that was all right.” Read about the life of Ambassador Terence A. Todman and his legacy of promoting diversity ahead of an event to honor him on February 1. @ShareAmerica has more on his extraordinary career: https://t.co/CcfqnQt7OE. https://t.co/pSxEDD9VKk

On this day 19 years ago, former President George W. Bush announced @PEPFAR. Through strong & sustained bipartisan American leadership and investment, the U.S. has helped transform the global #HIV response and delivered hope and healing to millions of people. #PEPFARAnniversary. https://t.co/NiDU1egxWY

The United States welcomes the Czech Republic’s decision to join other @NATO Allies in donating military equipment. This will help bolster Ukraine’s defensive capabilities in the face of Russian aggression. We are #StrongerTogether.

Today, the number of political prisoners held in Belarus surpassed 1,000. The United States will stand in solidarity with them and all Belarusians until they are free to build a country that respects the human rights and democratic aspirations of all. https://t.co/D86JUFX5Z3

The U.S. continues to lead on combatting the climate crisis through the Major Economies Forum. Today's ministerial reaffirms commitments made at COP26 & underscores the importance of continuing to push for increased ambition in the year, and decade, ahead. https://t.co/urQq5r3zB3

.@SecBlinken: There should be no doubt about our seriousness of purpose when it comes to diplomacy, and we’re acting with equal focus and force to bolster Ukraine’s defenses and prepare a swift, united response to further Russian aggression. More: https://t.co/Urj6L01HGd. https://t.co/ak7ewAxFbd

.@DeputySecState: It bears repeating, that it was Russia that invaded Ukraine in 2014 and occupies Crimea to this day. It is Russia that continues to fuel a war in eastern Ukraine that has claimed more than 14,000 lives and destroyed entire towns. https://t.co/JDybhuLuhC

.@SecBlinken: We’re open to dialogue, we prefer diplomacy, and we’re prepared to move forward where there is the possibility of communication and cooperation if Russia de-escalates its aggression toward Ukraine. https://t.co/nDiNJGGdLE
.@UnderSecStateP Victoria Nuland: “If Russia invades Ukraine, one way or another, Nord Stream 2 will not move forward.” https://t.co/hEGBrx6HJj

On #HolocaustRemembranceDay, we remember the six million Jews and millions of others who were killed during the #Holocaust. "Atrocities like the Holocaust don't just happen. They are allowed to happen. It's up to us to stop them." - @SecBlinken https://t.co/GJKOQp9nKV

.@SecBlinken: ​​We’ve laid out a diplomatic path. We’ve lined up steep consequences should Russia choose further aggression. We’ve stepped forward with more support for Ukraine’s security and economy. And we and our allies and partners are united across the board. https://t.co/QHf7hrPlXl
.@DeputySecState: The United States is united with the people of Ukraine. We remain committed to Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity. https://t.co/5otIACnfAI
.@SecBlinken: “We’re engaged in detailed conversations with our allies and partners about coordinating our response.” Full remarks: https://t.co/PKa717VNOX https://t.co/BX1qR59lRT

.@SecBlinken on the stakes of Russian aggression: If we allow these basic principles to be violated with impunity, then we are opening a Pandora’s box...It will take us back to a time of division, conflict, and worse, that so many of us labored for many years to move beyond. https://t.co/o1B6aWrDP9

.@SecBlinken: There are fundamental principles of international relations at stake, principles that were established after two world wars and a cold war in order to try to keep the peace, to try to provide security, to try to prevent the continent from falling back into conflict. https://t.co/3HRx48QsX7

Supporting the Haitian people remains a major priority for the United States. @DeputySecState Wendy Sherman participated in the High-Level Ministerial on Haiti hosted by @CanadaFP. Read the latest on how we are assisting the people of Haiti. https://t.co/NTrNEFlxeD

The United States has provided 400 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to over 110 countries and economies and provided nearly $20 billion in relief to vulnerable communities worldwide. Together, we can beat COVID-19. https://t.co/kNv7PMbmy2

.@SecBlinken on the COVID pandemic: We know from hard experience that as long as the virus is percolating anywhere, it may be developing new variants, and those variants may come back and defeat the defenses and remedies that we put in place. https://t.co/Zav3nppShI

.@StateDeptSpox: Russia’s surging of troops into Belarus is a cause for deep concern. We’ve also made clear to Belarus that if it allows its territory to be used for an attack on Ukraine, it would face a swift and decisive response from the U.S. and our allies and partners. https://t.co/15yb9ca1yK

.@SecBlinken on a way forward with Russia: There are several steps that we can take – all of us, Russia included – to increase transparency, to reduce risks, to advance arms control, to build trust. https://t.co/smAXd88aMs

.@SecBlinken met with Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov and shared the U.S. objective to determine whether Russia is ready to take a diplomatic path -- and that any renewed Russian invasion into Ukraine will be met with a swift and united response from the U.S., partners & allies. https://t.co/hG2cqRYtoM

The U.S., in partnership with #COVAX, delivered an additional 337,350 doses of the Johnson & Johnson COVID-19 vaccine to Bangladesh, building on the more than 28 million doses we previously donated. These vaccines are vital to saving lives in Bangladesh and around the world.

.@SecBlinken: If Russia invades and occupies Ukraine, what’s next? It may seem like a distant regional dispute or yet another example of Russian bullying. But at stake are principles that have made the world safer and more stable for decades. https://t.co/l3tq9TXwNG

.@SecBlinken: The United States and our European allies have repeatedly reached out to Russia with offers of diplomacy, in a spirit of reciprocity. So far, our good-faith gestures have been rebuffed. https://t.co/yH0NTCqp54

.@SecBlinken: No one should be surprised if Russia instigates a provocation or incident – then tries to use it to justify military intervention, hoping that by the time the world realizes the ruse, it’ll be too late. https://t.co/kVEhU7KpVi

.@SecBlinken: Russia has continued to escalate its threat toward Ukraine. It’s bigger than a conflict between two countries, and it’s bigger than a clash between Russia and NATO. It’s a crisis with global consequences. And it requires global attention and action. https://t.co/LDhBO6yuZc
Russia’s recent attempts to fill the media environment with mistruths on Ukraine are part of a longstanding pattern. Here are their major recurring disinformation themes: https://t.co/e0bzzou06Z
We are breaking down the truth about Russian disinformation. Get the details here: https://t.co/V1dKyGvoSf.

.@SecBlinken provides an update from Berlin on diplomatic dialogue to de-escalate tensions with Russia: “We’re here now to consult and coordinate with allies and partners on how to proceed...That unity gives us strength, a strength that Russia does not and cannot match.” https://t.co/vqt6C8iSRH

.@SecBlinken: We are fully committed to meaningful reciprocal dialogue with Russia, just as we are fully committed to consulting and coordinating with our allies and partners — including the European Union. We would far prefer a diplomatic path and a diplomatic solution. https://t.co/oUJN1ZFgF2

As @SecBlinken has made clear, the United States stands united with Ukraine. Learn more about our support for Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity and the U.S.-Ukraine relationship here: https://t.co/IRJZGZIEpv

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom is a human right; in fact, it goes to the heart of what it means to be human — to think freely, to follow our conscience, to change our beliefs if our hearts and minds lead us to do so, to express those beliefs in public and in private. https://t.co/WtwgujYpv4

Religious freedom is a key element of an open and stable society. On this Religious Freedom Day, we reaffirm our commitment to protecting and advancing the fundamental right of religious freedom, both at home and abroad. https://t.co/4dFTWJLBR8

.@SecBlinken: Religious freedom, like every human right, is universal.  All people, everywhere, are entitled to it no matter where they live, what they believe, or what they don’t believe. https://t.co/ZnfGaKqN7S

.@JakeSullivan46: We have been very clear with Russia on the costs and consequences of further military action or destabilization in Ukraine. https://t.co/Hf4WODnTKW
